DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
CLARE MORAN, Esq. MATERN LAW GROUP, PC, (310) 531-1900 cmoran@maternlawgroup.com for Plaintiff JANE ROE
SUSANNAH K. HOWARD, Esq., O'MELVENY & MYERS LLP,+1 415 984 8700, showard@omm.com, for Defendants Zendesk, Inc. and Matthew Ingebrigtsen.
Not Reported.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' MATTHEW INGEBRIGSTEN, ZENDESK, INC.'S demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

The FAC adequately pleads a cause of action for FEHA retaliation. It alleges "facts regarding the content" and timing of plaintiff's "complaints to Aaron Schilke and human resources." (FAC 6:23-7:4, 9:21-10:18, 4:12-6:22, 7:9-9:21, 10:20-12:14; 3/21/22 Order 2:22-24.) The FAC also alleges the timing of defendants' actions and their effect on plaintiff's employment. (FAC 4:12-6:22, 7:9-9:21, 10:20-12:14; 3/21/22 Order 3:11-13.) The FAC also alleges defendants' awareness of plaintiff's protected activities. (FAC 6:23-7:4, 9:21-10:18; 3/21/22 Order 3:18-20.)

The FAC adequately pleads a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. That defendant Ingebrigtsen made the harassing and misogynistic statements attributed to him is assumed at the pleading stage. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) These are not "trivialities," and whether they occurred and were "extreme"/"outrageous" is for adjudication. (Crouch v. Trinity Christian Ctr. of Santa Ana, Inc. (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 995, 1007; Duronslet v. County of Los Angeles (C.D. Cal. 2017) 266 F.Supp.3d 1213, 1219; 3/21/22 Order 4:26-28.) The same is true of defendants' other alleged misconduct. (FAC 4:11-5:2, 5:22-12:17, 23:23-24.) Further, causation is adequately pled. (Id. at 1:24-12:14, 23:23-24, 24:21-26.)

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
